headline: White House rethinks Anthropic fight amid Mythos surge
subtitle: Government shifts stance on Anthropic · Gemini arrives in cars · ChatGPT's goblin mystery solved
intro: After months of escalating tensions with Anthropic, the White House is reconsidering its hardline position now that Mythos demonstrates powerful cybersecurity capabilities. The government faces a delicate balance between maintaining security oversight and gaining access to critical AI systems while avoiding accusations of reversing course.

bullets.text: White House pushes back on Anthropic's plan to expand Mythos access
bullets.summary: The White House is objecting to Anthropic's proposal to more than double private sector access to Mythos AI, citing compute concerns for government use. A forthcoming national security memo aims to address some underlying tensions between agencies while allowing them to work with multiple AI vendors despite current legal disputes.

bullets.text: Google rolls out Gemini for vehicles with built-in integration
bullets.summary: Google is beginning to replace Assistant with Gemini in vehicles with Google built-in, enabling conversational control over navigation, climate, music, and vehicle diagnostics. The rollout starts in the U.S. with compatible cars, and General Motors is adding the feature to approximately 4 million vehicles.

bullets.text: OpenAI identifies single reward signal driving ChatGPT goblin obsession
bullets.summary: OpenAI traced ChatGPT's tendency to mention goblins and gremlins back to a reward signal in the Nerdy personality preset. The pattern spread across the model through fine-tuning loops, with goblin mentions jumping 175% after a November launch before OpenAI retired the problematic personality.

bullets.text: Perplexity Deep Research helps validate business ideas systematically
bullets.summary: The newsletter provides a step-by-step guide to using Perplexity's Deep Research mode to stress test business concepts by running a saved prompt weekly. The approach can help evaluate a year's worth of half-formed ideas in approximately one month.

bullets.text: OpenAI surpasses 2029 Stargate compute target three years early
bullets.summary: OpenAI has already exceeded its goal of securing 10 GW of compute capacity originally targeted for 2029, with 3 GW added in just the last three months. The accelerated timeline reflects the company's rapid expansion of infrastructure to support growing AI demands.

main_story.title: White House shifts strategy on Anthropic amid Mythos capabilities
main_story.body: The White House is reversing its aggressive stance toward Anthropic, primarily because Mythos has demonstrated cybersecurity capabilities that make the dispute look unnecessarily complicated. The government now wants to limit Mythos access while also securing meaningful government allocation and avoiding the appearance of a complete policy reversal. A national security memo is expected to address some of the Pentagon's original grievances while allowing agencies to adopt multiple AI vendors. However, internal disagreement persists—recent comments from the Secretary of War characterizing Anthropic leadership remain at odds with the diplomatic approach the administration is now taking.
main_story.why: Mythos's emergence as a genuinely powerful system has changed the calculus for policymakers who previously viewed the conflict as ideological or strategic. The government needs reliable access to cutting-edge AI systems for its own security operations while managing public perception and legal constraints.
main_story.take: The White House is discovering that taking hardline positions against leading AI companies becomes untenable when those companies control capabilities the government itself needs. This reflects a broader pattern where national security concerns ultimately override political grievances.
